content.full_text:
California 
~'Clir Political 
Practices Commission 
Jamie Foster 
241 Elaine Way 
Pismo Beach, CA 
93449 
Dear Mr. Foster: 
October 24, 1988 
Re: 
Our File No. I-88-398 
You have asked whether a counci1member has a conflict of 
interest concerning decisions to provide city funding for 
rebuilding the street where the counci1member lives. 
It 
appears that you have not been authorized by the counci1member 
to request this advice on his or her behalf. 
Accordingly, we 
cannot provide a specific response to your question. 
(See 
Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.) 
Enclosed is a copy of a pamphlet explaining the conf1ict-
of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act, a copy of 
In re Brown (1978) 4 FPPC Ops. 19, and a copy of an advice 
letter concerning a decision that may be similar to the one you 
have described. 
Also enclosed is a complaint form, which you 
may file with our Enforcement Division if you believe that a 
violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

By:

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 "J" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear FPPC, 
October 8, 1988 
Jamie Foster 
241 Etaine Way 
Pismo Beach, CA 93449 
I 
would 
be 
interested 
in 
your 
opinion 
regarding 
the 
following question: 
"Is 
there a Conflict of Interest when a Councilmember lives 
(owns 
the 
property) on a street that is 
being 
considered 
for 
rebuilding 
and t~e City could participate in the expense of 
the 
project (the City's portion is about $770,000)?" 
Some of the arguments I've heard of: 
1. Yes. There is direct financial benefit in that the property 
the Councilmember owns will go UP in value as a result of the 
street wor::Z. 
2. No. 
~Jhile the value of the property may go up, the 
Councilmember benefits no more than all the rest of the people 
on the street. 
3. No. The orocess began years ago. (Though the final approval to 
spend th~ money has not yet been acted upon.) 
What do you think? 
Thank you for taking the time to consider this matter. 
Sincerely, 
Jamie Foster

sections.question: Is there a Conflict of Interest when a Councilmember lives (owns the property) on a street that is being considered for rebuilding and the City could participate in the expense of the project (the City's portion is about $770,000)?
sections.conclusion: You have asked whether a councilmember has a conflict of interest concerning decisions to provide city funding for rebuilding the street where the councilmember lives. It appears that you have not been authorized by the councilmember to request this advice on his or her behalf. Accordingly, we cannot provide a specific response to your question. (See Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a councilmember has a conflict of interest in voting on city funding for rebuilding a street where the councilmember owns property, given the potential increase in property value.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide a specific response because the requester (Jamie Foster) was not authorized by the councilmember to request the advice on their behalf, pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (October 8, 1988) and FPPC declination response (October 24, 1988). Classified as declination because FPPC declined to provide substantive advice. No affirmative conclusion reached on the underlying conflict of interest question.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is there a Conflict of Interest when a Councilmember lives (owns the property) on a street that is being considered for rebuilding and the City could participate in the expense of the project (the City's portion is about $770,000)?

CONCLUSION: You have asked whether a councilmember has a conflict of interest concerning decisions to provide city funding for rebuilding the street where the councilmember lives. It appears that you have not been authorized by the councilmember to request this advice on his or her behalf. Accordingly, we cannot provide a specific response to your question. (See Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed.)

embedding.summary: Jamie Foster inquired whether a councilmember has a conflict of interest in voting on city funding for street rebuilding on a street where the councilmember owns property. The FPPC declined to provide specific advice because Foster lacked authorization from the councilmember to make the request.
